company_type: Specialty Chemicals - Flavors, Fragrances, Food Ingredients, Health & Biosciences

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company uses many different raw materials including essential oils, extracts and concentrates derived from fruits, vegetables, flowers, woods and other botanicals, animal products, raw fruits, organic chemicals and petroleum-based chemicals, gelatin, glycols, cellulose products, processed grains, guar, locust bean gum, organic vegetable oils, peels, saccharides, seaweed, soybeans, and sugars and yeasts. For certain ingredients and raw materials, the Company relies on a limited number of suppliers where there are not readily available alternatives.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions, geopolitical developments, climate-change events, natural disasters, public health crises, tariffs and trade wars may adversely affect procurement of raw materials and manufacturing. Environmental events may affect facilities, customers or suppliers and the availability, quality and pricing of raw materials. The Russia-Ukraine war has impacted sourcing of certain raw materials. Concentration risk exists for certain ingredients with limited alternative suppliers.

regulatory_risks: Compliance with FDA and equivalent international agencies regulating flavors, pharmaceutical excipients and ingredients in consumer products
EPA and equivalent international agencies regulating manufacturing facilities and fragrance products
OSHA and equivalent international agencies regulating working conditions
Chemical Registration/Notification authorities regulating chemicals (REACH regulations in EU and similar regulations in other countries)
Ban on microplastics adopted by European Commission
European Green Deal Chemicals Strategy for Sustainability (CSS) triggering updates to REACH, CLP, Cosmetic Regulation and Detergent Regulation
U.S. Department of Agriculture and equivalent international authorities regulations on labeling of consumer products
Increased regulatory scrutiny towards artificial or other ingredients and certain chemical substances
Data protection laws including GDPR in EU and CCPA in California
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and similar anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws in other countries
Competition and antitrust laws enforcement in U.S., EU and other jurisdictions
Sanctions and export controls (including on Russia, Belarus and occupied regions of Ukraine)
Environmental protection laws relating to air emissions, sewage discharges, use of hazardous materials, waste disposal practices
Product safety, quality, efficacy and environmental impact standards
False or deceptive advertising claims relating to efficacy, health benefits of functional food, nutraceuticals products
Tax legislation changes including OECD Pillar Two minimum tax rules
Inflation Reduction Act minimum book tax and excise tax on stock repurchases
Customs, tariffs, trade protection measures, import/export licensing requirements

sanction_risks: U.S., EU and other countries have imposed sanctions and export controls on Russia, Belarus and occupied regions of Ukraine
Company has limited export of ingredients to Russia, Belarus and occupied regions of Ukraine to only those permitted that meet essential needs

Imposition of or changes in customs, tariffs, trade protection measures, import/export licensing requirements and sanctions could adversely affect cost or ability to import raw materials or export products
US-China relations and escalating tensions between China and Taiwan mentioned as geopolitical developments
Israel-Hamas war and wider Middle East developments impacting operations in Israel and certain customers, local markets and suppliers
Russia-Ukraine war continues to impact sourcing of certain raw materials and operations in Russia and Ukraine
Compliance with sanctions laws is highly technical and failure to comply could result in material adverse effects
